vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are not identifiable as living specimens in the provided sequence. The pots contain only soil, debris (eggshells), and sensor hardware.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible; White rabbit anchor is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.P3: Empty soil surface, black pot, no anchor.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark, damp, and undisturbed throughout the sequence.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P3/P4 area).
biome_observations.hardware: Soil moisture sensor (v2.0.0) present in the yellow pot (P1).
biome_observations.desk_surface: The final image (CURRENT) shows a complete loss of visual data (grey/noise), indicating a camera failure or obstruction.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From T-5 to T-1, the biome remained static with no vegetative growth. The transition to CURRENT represents a total loss of visual monitoring capability.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional as a botanical environment. There is no evidence of plant life, only soil and inert objects.
vision_report.anomalies: The 'White Rabbit' anchor is missing from all images. The final image is a total visual occlusion/failure.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the registered botanical specimens. The pots contain only soil and sensor equipment. The sequence concludes with a total loss of visual input, rendering further monitoring impossible.
